report_name: Anubis Ransomware Hits Adriatic Port, Stealing Port Safety Plans in $10M Extortion Attempt

threat_actors.name: Anubis
threat_actors.description: A ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group that targeted a major port authority on the Adriatic Sea, demanding a $10 million ransom and exfiltrating sensitive data including port safety plans. The group is distinct from the older Android malware of the same name.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration of sensitive port security documents.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Used to gain initial access to the port authority's network by targeting staff with a malicious link in an email, which likely led to credential harvesting.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers abused insecurely configured Microsoft 365 and Azure cloud accounts to move laterally and escalate privileges within the victim's hybrid environment.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Sensitive files, including port safety and security plans, were accessed and exfiltrated from cloud repositories like SharePoint after gaining privileged cloud access.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Anubis ransomware payload was deployed across the port's IT network to encrypt systems, causing operational disruption.

malware.name: Anubis Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the Anubis group to cripple the port's IT systems and encrypt data as part of a $10 million extortion attempt. It operates on a ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) model.

tools.name: rclone.exe
tools.description: The article's cyber observables section lists 'rclone.exe', a command-line tool for managing cloud storage, which was likely used for data exfiltration from the compromised cloud accounts.

identities.name: Adriatic Port Authority
identities.description: A major port authority on the Adriatic Sea targeted by the Anubis ransomware group, resulting in operational disruption and the theft of sensitive port safety plans.

indicators.name: Rclone Execution for Potential Data Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: Rclone Execution for Potential Data Exfiltration
id: 9c5b1a3d-4e8f-4b1a-8c7d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of rclone.exe, a tool that can be used for legitimate data synchronization but is also frequently abused by threat actors for data exfiltration from cloud storage.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/anubis-ransomware-adriatic-port/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-15
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.exfiltration
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\rclone.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'copy'
      - 'sync'
      - 'move'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of rclone by administrators for data backup or migration.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects the execution of rclone.exe, a tool often used by adversaries for data exfiltration from cloud environments, as noted in the report's observables.

indicators.name: Anomalous Cloud Account Login
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Cloud Account Login Pattern
id: 8b4a0c9e-3d7f-4a0b-9c6d-8e7f6a5b4c3d
status: experimental
description: Detects anomalous cloud account login patterns, such as impossible travel, which could indicate compromised credentials as seen in the Anubis attack on the Adriatic port.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/anubis-ransomware-adriatic-port/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-15
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
  - attack.initial_access
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    ResultType: 0
    RiskLevelDuringSignIn: 'high'
    RiskState: 'atRisk'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate user travel, use of VPNs.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects anomalous cloud account logins, such as impossible travel, which was a likely component of the lateral movement and privilege escalation phase using compromised cloud accounts.

indicators.name: Rclone Tool Filename
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Rclone Tool Filename Detection
id: 7a3b9c8d-2e6f-490a-8b5c-7d6e5f4a3b2c
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of the rclone.exe file, a tool often abused by threat actors for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/anubis-ransomware-adriatic-port/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-15
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.exfiltration
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\rclone.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate installations of rclone.
level: low
indicators.description: Detects the presence of the file 'rclone.exe', a tool noted in the report's observables and often used for data exfiltration.

relationships.source_name: Rclone Execution for Potential Data Exfiltration
